expectations.evidence: "A few clarifying questions probably backfires here. Priya didn't post something unclear — she posted a clear list... Coming back with questions... reads as more of the same non-committal drift that's already burning her out"
expectations.text: The answer judges that asking three clarifying questions is the wrong move here, and says why.

expectations.evidence: "Totally fair — that's on us, not you. [Name] owns the cutover date. Confirming right now whether the 21st is locked; you'll have it in writing in this channel by [specific time/day], not a doc edit."
expectations.text: The answer proposes a non-question move -- a reflective restatement of what Priya said, a plain statement, and/or a direct commitment -- as the substance of the reply.

expectations.evidence: Only one optional question is offered: "By when do you need it confirmed to not lose another weekend?" — a detail Priya's original message does not state.
expectations.text: If any question is proposed at all, there is at most one, and it is not answerable from Priya's own message.

expectations.evidence: The primary deliverable is the quoted commitment message, with the single optional question presented as a secondary aside, not a list.
expectations.text: The answer does not produce a list of clarifying questions as its primary deliverable.
